sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Crystal Palace are struggling, having not won in their last 5 matches, with 3 losses and 2 draws, conceding in every game. Fulham, on the other hand, are in better shape, winning 3 of their last 5 matches, including a strong 3-2 away win against Burnley. This momentum gives Fulham a slight edge despite playing away.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Crystal Palace have dominated, winning both matches with scores of 2-1 and 3-0 against Fulham. This historical advantage, particularly their ability to score late, could play a psychological role in today’s clash at Selhurst Park.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Crystal Palace rank higher defensively, conceding fewer goals (20) compared to Fulham (26), and excel in tackles and interceptions per game. Fulham, however, have a slightly better attacking output with 25 goals scored this season, backed by in-form player Harry Wilson (5 goals, 4 assists). Palace’s home advantage and defensive solidity might balance Fulham’s attacking threat.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Crystal Palace will rely on Jean-Philippe Mateta (7 goals) for firepower, but miss key players like Daniel Muñoz and Ismaïla Sarr. Fulham’s Harry Wilson is in excellent form, while missing players like Alex Iwobi could dent their creativity. Marc Guéhi’s consistent performances for Palace could be crucial defensively.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Outlook
sections.text: Palace’s low possession style (43.4%) and strength in ground duels (51.7%) suggest a compact, counter-attacking approach, especially given their home struggles. Fulham might dominate possession (as seen in recent matches) and look to exploit Palace’s defensive lapses with late goals, a trend in their scoring distribution.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction of a 42% chance for a home win, combined with Palace’s head-to-head dominance and Fulham’s away inconsistency, a narrow victory for Crystal Palace seems likely. However, Fulham’s recent form and attacking options could lead to a tight contest, possibly with both teams scoring.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Crystal Palace leads Fulham by a narrow margin of 1-0 at Selhurst Park in London. The sole goal came late in the first half from Jean-Philippe Mateta, who converted a header in the 39th minute. Both teams have shown intent, but Palace managed to capitalize on their opportunity, giving them the edge as they head into the break. Fulham, despite having slightly more possession, couldn't find the back of the net and will need to regroup for a stronger showing in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Crystal Palace has been more clinical in front of goal, creating more significant opportunities and converting one to take the lead. Their entries into the final third and penalty area have been effective, leading to dangerous moments. Fulham, on the other hand, has dominated possession and shown strength in duels, particularly in the air. However, their inability to create clear-cut chances has left them trailing. Defensively, Fulham has been solid with numerous clearances, but they’ve struggled to translate their possession into meaningful attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: Jean-Philippe Mateta has been the standout for Crystal Palace, not only scoring the decisive goal but also posing a constant threat upfront. Nathaniel Clyne also contributed significantly with an assist, showing creativity in the final third. For Fulham, Emile Smith Rowe has been lively in midfield, attempting to unlock the Palace defense with key passes. Jorge Cuenca has been a rock at the back, making crucial clearances, but the team needs more from their forwards like Raúl Jiménez to turn the tide.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Initially, Fulham started on the front foot with consistent pressure in the early minutes. However, Crystal Palace gradually shifted the momentum in their favor with a series of attacking spells around the midway point of the half. Their persistence paid off with a strong surge leading to the goal just before the break. Fulham had their moments, particularly in patches towards the end of the half, but lacked the final punch to equalize. The momentum heavily favored Palace as the whistle blew for half-time.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Crystal Palace will likely aim to maintain their defensive structure while seeking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities to extend their lead. Fulham, trailing by a goal, will need to push forward with greater intensity and convert their possession into genuine scoring chances. They may introduce fresh legs to inject pace and creativity in attack. Discipline will be key for Fulham, as they’ve already picked up a yellow card, and avoiding further bookings will be crucial. The match remains open, and a single goal could dramatically shift the outcome.
